commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Is there any thing of which it may be said, See, this is new. it has been already of old time, which was before us. Such vicissitudes have occurred before, though we must not infer that the world is eternal; or that there have been many others before this, as Origen would suppose. ) (Calmet) Men's souls, which are created daily, are nevertheless of the same sort as Adam's was; and creatures proceed from others of the same species, which have been from the beginning. Thomas Aquinas p. ) (Worthington) Natural and moral things continue much the same. (Menochius).
